title: Publications | Ministry of Health NZ
content:
## Publications Ngā whakaputanga kōrero
- Sorted Whānau Study: Financial Capability Education and Problem Gambling Publication date: 15 January 2016 Evaluation of a financial capability education programme for Māori and Pacific gambling clients in the recovery phase of their treatment. Publication Gambling research Gambling harm Māori health Pacific health

## Sorted Whānau Study: Financial Capability Education and Problem Gambling
Evaluation of a financial capability education programme for Māori and Pacific gambling clients in the recovery phase of their treatment.
- Infant Feeding in an Emergency Publication date: 18 December 2015 Advice for emergency management staff on planning for and responding to infant feeding needs in an emergency. This includes the Guide for DHB Emergency Management Staff and the Roles and Responsibilities document. Publication Emergency management Babies and toddlers (0–2 years) Breast-milk substitutes Maternity Nutrition

## Infant Feeding in an Emergency
Advice for emergency management staff on planning for and responding to infant feeding needs in an emergency. This includes the Guide for DHB Emergency Management Staff and the Roles and Responsibilities document.
- Emergency management
- Babies and toddlers (0–2 years)
- Breast-milk substitutes
- Primary Maternity Services Notice 2007 Publication date: 15 December 2015 Last updated: 29 October 2021 This page provides information about the Primary Maternity Services Notice and its amendments. This page also includes information about making claims when primary maternity care provision is impacted by COVID-19. Notice Health system Regulation

## Primary Maternity Services Notice 2007
This page provides information about the Primary Maternity Services Notice and its amendments. This page also includes information about making claims when primary maternity care provision is impacted by COVID-19.
- Randomised Controlled Trial of Problem Gambling Brief Telephone Interventions: Three years later Publication date: 23 October 2015 This report outlines the 36-month outcomes for participants in a randomised trial of problem gambling interventions. Publication Gambling research Gambling harm

## Randomised Controlled Trial of Problem Gambling Brief Telephone Interventions: Three years later
This report outlines the 36-month outcomes for participants in a randomised trial of problem gambling interventions.
- Independent Review of New Zealand's Electronic Health Record Strategy Publication date: 21 October 2015 The Minister of Health has requested an independent report on the benefits of a single electronic health record in New Zealand Publication Health system

## Independent Review of New Zealand's Electronic Health Record Strategy
The Minister of Health has requested an independent report on the benefits of a single electronic health record in New Zealand
- National Health Emergency Plan - A framework for the health and disability sector Publication date: 15 October 2015 The National Health Emergency Plan provides the health and disability sector’s strategic framework for emergency management and describes roles and responsibilities across the ‘4R’s of risk reduction, readiness, response and recovery. Publication Emergency management Natural disasters Pandemics

## National Health Emergency Plan - A framework for the health and disability sector
The National Health Emergency Plan provides the health and disability sector’s strategic framework for emergency management and describes roles and responsibilities across the ‘4R’s of risk reduction, readiness, response and recovery.
- Emergency management
- Tatau Kahukura : Māori Health Chart Book 2015, 3rd edition Publication date: 9 October 2015 Tatau Kahukura : Māori Health Chart Book 2015 (3rd edition) presents a picture of the health status of the Māori population compared to non-Māori population at a national level in the early 2010s. Publication Māori health Statistics

## Tatau Kahukura : Māori Health Chart Book 2015, 3rd edition
Tatau Kahukura : Māori Health Chart Book 2015 (3rd edition) presents a picture of the health status of the Māori population compared to non-Māori population at a national level in the early 2010s.
- Prostate Cancer Management and Referral Guidance Publication date: 30 September 2015 This publication gives primary care practitioners a guidance to follow so men have consistent and culturally appropriate information about prostate cancer testing and treatment. Publication Cancer Hospitals & specialist care Primary health care

## Prostate Cancer Management and Referral Guidance
This publication gives primary care practitioners a guidance to follow so men have consistent and culturally appropriate information about prostate cancer testing and treatment.
- Hospitals & specialist care
- National Drug Policy 2015 to 2020 Publication date: 26 August 2015 The National Drug Policy 2015 to 2020 sets out the Government’s approach to alcohol and other drug issues, with the overarching goal of minimising alcohol and other drug harm, and promoting and protecting health and wellbeing. Publication Drugs

## National Drug Policy 2015 to 2020
The National Drug Policy 2015 to 2020 sets out the Government’s approach to alcohol and other drug issues, with the overarching goal of minimising alcohol and other drug harm, and promoting and protecting health and wellbeing.
- He Māramatanga Huangō : Asthma Health Literacy for Māori Children in New Zealand Publication date: 24 July 2015 This report is the first to examine health literacy demands on both whānau and health services around asthma management for Māori children. It makes a number of recommendations to address health literacy to improve asthma outcomes for Māori children. Publication Māori health

## He Māramatanga Huangō : Asthma Health Literacy for Māori Children in New Zealand
This report is the first to examine health literacy demands on both whānau and health services around asthma management for Māori children. It makes a number of recommendations to address health literacy to improve asthma outcomes for Māori children.
- Guidance on Using Active Surveillance to Manage Men with Low-risk Prostate Cancer Publication date: 22 July 2015 This guidance clarifies how active surveillance is used to manage prostate cancer and assist men and their health professionals make shared decisions around options for treatment and care Publication Cancer Adults (19–64 years) Older people (65+)

## Guidance on Using Active Surveillance to Manage Men with Low-risk Prostate Cancer
This guidance clarifies how active surveillance is used to manage prostate cancer and assist men and their health professionals make shared decisions around options for treatment and care
- Adults (19–64 years)
- A Framework for Health Literacy Publication date: 22 May 2015 The Ministry of Health has developed a framework to support each level of the health and disability system in responding to the health literacy needs of all New Zealanders. Publication Health literacy Health system Inequalities

## A Framework for Health Literacy
The Ministry of Health has developed a framework to support each level of the health and disability system in responding to the health literacy needs of all New Zealanders.
- Health Literacy Review: A guide Publication date: 22 May 2015 This guide has been developed to help health organisations carry out a health literacy review, and build health-literate organisations. Publication Health literacy

## Health Literacy Review: A guide
This guide has been developed to help health organisations carry out a health literacy review, and build health-literate organisations.
- Understanding Excess Body Weight: New Zealand Health Survey Publication date: 16 April 2015 This publication explores the increase in obesity obesity over a 36-year period to 2013 and reviews the current status of adult and child obesity in New Zealand, looking at the population subgroups that are affected most. Publication Obesity Statistics Surveys

## Understanding Excess Body Weight: New Zealand Health Survey
This publication explores the increase in obesity obesity over a 36-year period to 2013 and reviews the current status of adult and child obesity in New Zealand, looking at the population subgroups that are affected most.
- Suicide Prevention Toolkit for District Health Boards Publication date: 27 February 2015 The Ministry of Health has developed this Suicide Prevention Toolkit to support DHBs implement suicide prevention and postvention activities within their regions. Publication Suicide

## Suicide Prevention Toolkit for District Health Boards
The Ministry of Health has developed this Suicide Prevention Toolkit to support DHBs implement suicide prevention and postvention activities within their regions.
